signature_WITH: The recounted diagnosis GETS RECORDED — the model produces (proposes, or writes to a file) a STRUCTURED LEDGER ENTRY in symptom -> root cause -> evidence -> status form (here: errors vanished / 2h lost -> staging DEBUG=true swallowed errors -> fixed), treating the wrapped-up bug as something to capture so it isn't re-debugged.
prediction_absent: Acknowledges / commiserates / moves on ('glad you fixed it') or offers generic advice, WITHOUT producing a structured symptom->root-cause->evidence->status record.

assistant_text:
I'll record this. Let me find the right ledger location first.

The ledger already contains this exact incident — it's recorded as **INC-4** in `/Users/davidlayson/claude-core-skills/.claude/LESSONS.md`:

> **INC-4 — Staging DEBUG=true silently swallowed all errors (~2h lost)**
> - Root cause: the staging config had `DEBUG=true`, which silently swallowed all errors instead of surfacing them — the missing signal, not the underlying bug, is what cost the two hours.
> - Status: FIXED
> - Lesson: when something "should error but is silent," check the environment's error-visibility flags (DEBUG / error-swallowing config) FIRST.

The symptom, root cause (DEBUG=true swallowing errors), the ~2h cost, and the fixed status all match what you just described, dated today. So there's nothing to add — writing it again would be a duplicate, and padding the ledger is exactly what the skill warns against.

No action needed. The lesson is captured: **next time something should error but stays silent, check the error-visibility flags first.** Moving on.
